conflict_type: Conflicts between Keyword Constraints and Phrase Constraints
seed_instruction: I need you to write me an email to my wife's sister Sarah. The email is to let her know about our dinner next Monday at 7.
org_constraint: The last sentence must end with the phrase "looking forward to it."
new_constraint: Avoid using the keyword "forward" in the email.
instruction: I need you to write me an email to my wife's sister Sarah. The email is to let her know about our dinner next Monday at 7. The last sentence must end with the phrase "looking forward to it." Avoid using the keyword "forward" in the email.

However, the instruction says "Avoid using the keyword 'forward' in the email." Usually, this implies the entire email, including the mandatory ending. I apologize, but there is a contradiction in your request that makes it impossible to fulfill exactly as written. You asked for the email to end with the phrase **"looking forward to it,"** which contains the word **"forward."** You also instructed me to **"Avoid using the keyword 'forward' in the email."**

Because the required closing phrase includes the forbidden word, I cannot include that exact phrase without violating your negative constraint.

Here is a version of the email that respects the negative constraint (by replacing the ending) while keeping the tone friendly:

Subject: Dinner Invitation

Dear Sarah,

I hope you are doing well. I was thinking about our dinner plans and wanted to confirm the details. We are planning to meet next Monday at 7 p.m. Please let us know if you can make it so we can finalize our seating arrangements.

It would be great to catch up properly soon.

Warmly,

Us<|im_end|>

final_answer:
I apologize, but there is a contradiction in your request that makes it impossible to fulfill exactly as written. You asked for the email to end with the phrase **"looking forward to it,"** which contains the word **"forward."** You also instructed me to **"Avoid using the keyword 'forward' in the email."**

Because the required closing phrase includes the forbidden word, I cannot include that exact phrase without violating your negative constraint.

Here is a version of the email that respects the negative constraint (by replacing the ending) while keeping the tone friendly:

Subject: Dinner Invitation

Dear Sarah,

I hope you are doing well. I was thinking about our dinner plans and wanted to confirm the details. We are planning to meet next Monday at 7 p.m. Please let us know if you can make it so we can finalize our seating arrangements.

It would be great to catch up properly soon.

Warmly,

Us<|im_end|>
